what_it_does: CLI script that wires an isolated obsidian-overlord substrate instance to a local perplexity-ai/pplx-embed-context-v1-4b OpenAI-compatible embedding service, registers a claude-sessions vault over a markdown transcript corpus, indexes it into a cosine Chroma collection, and provides semantic search with optional cross-encoder reranking or a two-stage JSON candidate export.
observations: Explicitly labeled as a throwaway interim until a real memory system is built elsewhere.
Uses a dedicated SUBSTRATE_MCP_STATE_DIR to keep the pplx int8 embedding space isolated from the default bge-m3 substrate state.
Configures provider openai and reuses the ollama_base_url field as the generic base_url for the local pplx endpoint.
Chroma collection is created with hnsw:space=cosine to match the model card's cosine requirement.
Implements setup, index, status, search, and all subcommands with argparse.
index supports --full reset by deleting the Chroma collection and clearing address sha256 values.
search defaults to embedding-only results; --rerank enables a cross-encoder rerank step imported from ops/rerank.py.
--json on search dumps the candidate pool for out-of-process reranking across Python environments.
Hardcodes EXPECT_DIM=2560 but never references it in the executable code.
Sets os.environ[SUBSTRATE_MCP_STATE_DIR] before importing substrate_mcp.config so the library loads the isolated state.
standouts: Isolated throwaway substrate wiring for a separate int8 embedding model with documented rationale.
Optional rerank seam with both in-process and two-stage JSON-bridge modes.
Fail-loud behavior: sys.exit on missing corpus, unregistered vault, unreachable embedder, or empty index.
conventions: Follows the repo's FAIL LOUD law by exiting non-zero on first real error.
Uses environment variables for override paths and endpoints.
Uses argparse subparsers for CLI commands.
Imports substrate_mcp modules after sys.path manipulation, marked with noqa E402.
Breaks the ollama_base_url naming convention by using it as a generic embedding base_url, documented in the module docstring.
Defines a nested helper _add_rerank_flags inside main and reuses it for two subparsers.
concerns: EXPECT_DIM is declared but unused, so the dimension check is not enforced.
index --full uses action=store_true with default=True, making full reindex the only behavior and the flag redundant.
cmd_search imports rerank.Reranker at runtime; if ops/rerank.py is missing the command fails only when rerank is requested.
Derives the health endpoint by stripping /v1 from BASE_URL, which assumes a fixed path shape.
Calls chroma._client.delete_collection, relying on a private Chroma API.
Direct SQL DELETE/UPDATE in cmd_index is sensitive to schema changes.
notes: A throwaway but carefully isolated wiring harness that lets Claude session transcripts ride the same substrate query surface as the rest of the Company, while experimenting with a rerank seam.
questions: Where is ops/rerank.py located and what is the exact Reranker interface?
Why is EXPECT_DIM defined if it is never used?
What chunking and metadata strategy does scanner_mod.scan_vault apply to these transcripts?
What are the batch size and rate limits of the local pplx embedder service?
What is the real memory system being built elsewhere and when will it replace this script?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it configures an isolated pplx-backed substrate, registers and indexes the claude-sessions vault, and exposes search with optional reranking.
apparent_themes: Throwaway interim tooling with clear lifecycle intent
Pluggable rerank seam and multi-environment pipeline
intention_signals: The file is trying to give a specific transcript corpus the full substrate query surface without contaminating the main embedding store, and to prototype a clean seam where embedding retrieval can be optionally re-ranked by a cross-encoder running in a different environment.
novelty: It creates a dedicated, rationale-documented substrate instance for an int8 unnormalized perplexity embedding model and includes a two-stage JSON rerank bridge to work around missing torch/chromadb in the overlord venv.
contribution: A self-contained CLI pattern for pointing the substrate at a different local embedding endpoint, registering a markdown vault, indexing it, querying it, and optionally exporting candidates for reranking elsewhere; it contributes the wiring logic and the rerank seam hook as an experimental integration path.
summary_for_embedding: ops/wire_substrate_claude_sessions.py is a Python CLI that configures an isolated obsidian-overlord substrate backed by a local perplexity embedding service, registers a claude-sessions vault over a markdown transcript corpus, indexes it into a cosine Chroma collection, and supports semantic search with optional cross-encoder reranking or JSON candidate export.
intention_for_embedding: Intended as a temporary, isolated substrate-backed search path for Claude session transcripts while a permanent memory system is built elsewhere.

symbols.does: Checks that the local pplx embedding service /health endpoint returns status ok, exiting loudly on failure.
symbols.description: Performs a fail-loud connectivity check against the embedder health endpoint before any operation that requires embeddings, returning the health JSON on success.

symbols.does: Loads the substrate Config from the isolated SUBSTRATE_MCP_STATE_DIR.
symbols.description: Thin wrapper around config_mod.load that always uses the dedicated state directory set earlier in the module.

symbols.does: Configures the substrate for the pplx embedder and registers the claude-sessions vault if not already present.
symbols.description: Validates the corpus directory and markdown files, sets provider/model/base_url to the local pplx endpoint, persists the config, and adds the vault using config_mod.add_vault.

symbols.does: Scans the claude-sessions corpus and embeds its chunks into the isolated Chroma cosine collection.
symbols.description: Verifies wiring and embedder health, optionally resets the collection and database state with --full, then calls scanner_mod.scan_vault to chunk and embed the vault and reports file/chunk/vector counts.

symbols.does: Prints configuration and indexed counts for the claude-sessions vault.
symbols.description: Loads the config and database, reports provider/model/base_url, vault list, and counts of files, chunks, embedded chunks, and Chroma vectors.

symbols.does: Formats a single search result for terminal output, including rank, address, cosine distance, rerank score, and text snippet.
symbols.description: Helper used by cmd_search to render hits consistently in both embedding-only and reranked views.

symbols.does: Runs semantic search over the claude-sessions vault, with optional reranking or JSON candidate export.
symbols.description: Queries the Chroma collection, supports an embedding-only path, an in-process cross-encoder rerank path via ops/rerank.py, and a --json path that dumps candidates for two-stage reranking in another environment.

symbols.does: Chains setup, index, and search in one invocation.
symbols.description: Convenience command that runs the full pipeline from configuration through indexing to query execution.

symbols.does: Builds the argparse CLI and dispatches to the appropriate subcommand function.
symbols.description: Defines subparsers for setup, index, status, search, and all, attaches rerank flags where relevant, parses arguments, and calls the registered handler.

symbols.does: Adds optional rerank-related arguments to a subparser.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside main that attaches --rerank, --reranker, --fetch, and --json flags to both the search and all subparsers.
